channel.title: Resources on Esplanade Psychological Services
channel.description: Recent content in Resources on Esplanade Psychological Services

item.title: ADHD vs. Anxiety: Why They Can Look Similar
item.description: Difficulty focusing can be caused by ADHD, anxiety, depression, learning differences, sleep problems, or a combination of factors. Why this matters When the cause is unclear, supports can miss the mark. A comprehensive evaluation helps differentiate contributors and provides targeted recommendations. What we consider Developmental and school history Attention and executive functioning profile Learning and academic skills (when relevant) Emotional functioning and stress Context: school demands, expectations, and coping strategies If you’re seeking clarity around attention and performance, start here:

item.title: When to Seek a Neuropsychological Evaluation
item.description: A neuropsychological evaluation can be helpful when a student’s day-to-day functioning or school performance isn’t matching their effort or potential. Common reasons families reach out Persistent academic struggles (reading, writing, math) Attention or executive functioning challenges (organization, time management) Large variability across subjects or skills Emotional symptoms affecting school performance (anxiety, mood) Medical or neurological history (e.g., concussion/TBI) Need for clearer recommendations for IEP/504 planning or accommodations If you’re unsure what type of evaluation is the best fit, we can help you decide.
